channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden undergoes radiation therapy for cancer, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Joe Biden was diagnosed with aggressive prostate cancer four months ago and is now receiving radiation therapy as part of his treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jared Kushner reenters as key player in Middle East peace push
item.description: Jared Kushner, previously sidelined from President Trump's second administration, reemerged as a key envoy for the Middle East peace efforts, leveraging his influence and business ties in the region.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Biden receiving radiation, hormone therapy for prostate cancer
item.description: In May, Biden's postpresidential office announced that he had been diagnosed with prostate cancer and that it had spread to his bone.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dan Rosen sworn in as Minnesota U.S. Attorney
item.description: Minnesota's new U.S. Attorney was sworn in Friday during a private ceremony at the federal courthouse in Minneapolis. President Donald Trump nominated Dan Rosen in May to take over for Andy Luger.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: President Donald Trump is in 'exceptional health,' his doctor says, after visit to Walter Reed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is in “exceptional health," his physician said Friday after he underwent a checkup that included lab tests and preventive health assessments at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida's Attorney General race heats up
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — President Donald Trump officially put his support behind current Attorney General James Uthmeier, who hopes to keep his seat in office. That election will happen next year, with a challenger now in the race hoping to replace him. After being appointed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Toropin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What we know about the fraud case against New York attorney general Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James, a longtime political foe of President Donald Trump, was indicted by a federal grand jury Thursday on mortgage fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: BY PHILIP MARCELO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Republican Jensen makes another try for Minnesota governor
item.description: MPR News politics editor talked with Republican candidate Scott Jensen about his second bid for governor. Then, a look at how farmers are coping with trade barriers as harvest time is here.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Watch: Melania Trump set to make announcement
item.description: First lady Melania Trump gave an announcement Friday morning from the White House. Her remarks came after Amazon announced Thursday that its "Melania" documentary will be released in theaters on Jan. 30, 2026, and then later be available on its Prime Video platform.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: China hits US ships with retaliatory port fees before trade talks
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — China has hit U.S.-owned vessels docking in the country with tit-for-tat port fees, in response to the American government’s planned port fees on Chinese ships, expanding a string of retaliatory measures before trade talks between U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cindy McCain suffers a mild stroke and will take leave from World Food Program while recovering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Cindy McCain, the widow of Sen. John McCain and head of the U.N. World Food Program, suffered a mild stroke this week and is said to be recovering “well," according to a press release Thursday from the humanitarian organization.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Candidates in Virginia governor's debate clash over shutdown and violent rhetoric
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Republican Winsome Earle-Sears and Democrat Abigail Spanberger faced off for the first and only time on the debate stage Thursday night in Virginia's high-stakes gubernatorial race.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, STEVE PEOPLES and THOMAS BEAUONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks National Guard deployment in Illinois for 2 weeks
item.description: A judge has blocked the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area for two weeks, finding no substantial evidence that a “danger of rebellion” is brewing in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Travelers continue to endure delays amid government shutdown
item.description: On Thursday, Duffy told Fox Business that he may fire air traffic controllers who haven't shown up for work.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Letitia James indicted on federal fraud charges, denies wrongdoing
item.description: Letitia James denied all wrongdoing and accused President Donald Trump of weaponizing the justice system.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgment
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Vanity Fair’s Zohran Mamdani 'cover' not quite what it seems
item.description: While the story was indeed a cover — it was a “digital” cover, and it was directly assigned by the new editor Mark Guiducci.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Big jumps coming to Minnesota property taxes for some
item.description: As local governments set their budgets, many are leaning on higher property taxes to deal with inflation, new state requirements or drawdowns in federal program backing.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Former Minn. governors Carlson, Dayton tell court National Guard deployment is unconstitutional
item.description: Former governors Arne Carlson and Mark Dayton are among a group of bipartisan governors who are asking to support Illinois leaders in their lawsuit against the Trump administration’s deployment of National Guard troops to Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Trump Cabinet meeting focuses on shutdown, crime
item.description: The president is said to be considering invoking the Insurrection Act as he sends National Guard troops to Portland and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s use of National Guard strays from role as ‘minutemen,’ military experts say
item.description: Military experts say they also worry how these new deployments will affect recruitment and public trust.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Oceanside school district announces support for Prop 50
item.description: In a unanimous vote, the Oceanside Unified School District (OUSD) voted to support California Proposition 50 on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Walz reframes condition for calling special session on
item.description: The statement Wednesday seemed to walk back prior comments the DFLer made about bringing lawmakers back to St. Paul with or without an agreement on a special session.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers clash over property tax vs. insurance priorities
item.description: State lawmakers returned to Tallahassee this week with affordability concerns top of mind, as they gear up for next session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Govs. group faces division on Trump's troop deployment
item.description: The Democratic governors of California and Illinois are threatening to leave the National Governors Association because of its silence on President Donald Trump’s deployment of National Guard troops across state lines despite objections from the receiving states’ leaders.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida colleges must rename campus streets after Charlie Kirk under new bill
item.description: A proposed bill threatens to withhold funding from Florida colleges and universities if they do not rename campus roadways after slain conservative political commentator Charlie Kirk.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats take legal aim at ‘the Radical Left’ language during shutdown
item.description: Democrats and a federal union argue that Trump administration language posted on federal agency websites and some emails blaming a shutdown on the “Radical Left Democrats” violates a 1939 federal law.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler, MPR News

item.title: White House floats no back pay for workers despite law
item.description: A new draft White House memo suggests a 2019 law signed by President Trump that guarantees that federal employees get paid after a shutdown ends would not apply to furloughed workers.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: How the government shutdown is affecting air travel
item.description: A dozen facilities saw air traffic control shortages on Monday, delaying flights at several airports. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy blamed “a slight tick-up in sick calls” due to the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Future of Florida's congressional redistricting uncertain
item.description: As other states continue in the push to redraw congressional maps, Florida's role in this fight has diminished in recent weeks, at least publicly. However, groups against the move are still on defense as the state awaits the next steps.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump talks 'love' and 'conflict' with Carney during Canadian prime minister's visit
item.description: President Donald Trump is describing the relationship between the United States and Canada as having “mutual love” but also “natural conflict.” On Tuesday, he met with Prime Minister Mark Carney in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems skeptical about state bans on 'conversion therapy' for LBGTQ+ kids
item.description: A majority of Supreme Court justices seem to be leaning in favor of a Christian counselor who is challenging bans on LGBTQ+ “conversion therapy” for kids as a violation of her First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Psychiatrists call for RFK Jr. to be replaced as health secretary
item.description: Two groups are calling for new leadership at HHS after Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s actions on substance abuse treatment and mental health medications, among other issues.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee, MPR News

item.title: Bondi set to give testimony to Congress at first hearing since Comey indictment
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi is set to testify before a Senate panel Tuesday amid mounting concerns that the DOJ under her leadership is being weaponized to go after President Trump's perceived enemies.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s power to deploy National Guard, explained
item.description: President Trump is bucking tradition and legal precedent in pushing to deploy the National Guard to Democratic-led cities like Portland, Oregon, and Chicago due to what he says is rampant crime and to support his crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz and Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Voting is underway in California on new maps that could swing US House control, check Trump's power
item.description: The midterm elections might be a year away, but the fight for control of the U.S. House is underway in California.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, Fox 5
